famous quote:
"Aslan is a lion- the Lion, the great Lion." "Ooh" said Susan. "I'd thought he was a man. Is he-quite safe? I shall feel rather nervous about meeting a lion"..."Safe?" said Mr Beaver ..."Who said anything about safe? 'Course he isn't safe. But he's good. He's the King, I tell you."
-- C.S. Lewis (The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe (Chronicles of Narnia, #1))
Interview Text:
Will:

(part 1)

My friend Claire read my Ethiopia book and passed it on to her brother. He found me on Facebook and told me that he liked it. Then he mentioned he had seen similar miracles here in America. Really?! So I interviewed him.

First I asked him to tell about a couple of the miracles, and then to tell us what helps him hear from God.

He had a friend in his church named Tim who was seeing miracles and he asked if Will wanted to be part of it. Tim didn't want to be 'the guy' but instead wanted to empower others to do it. The idea was you don't need a seminary degree to access the Spirit.

So Will learned from Tim. The first time Will prayed with someone, it was for pain in their leg. Will tapped it while saying, "Pain get out, in the name of Jesus." The person froze and said, "How'd you do that?" Their pain had left.

Then in a prayer meeting one time, Tim asked if they could pray for Will's shoulders. Will's shoulders had been crooked for a long time, at least an inch difference in height. When Tim prayed they were straightened. When Will is struggling with doubt sometimes, he looks in the mirror and asks himself, "What are you doubting for?" And that visual reminder is more powerful than if he had been healed of some pain that he could easily forget about.

As he pursued it, Will started to hear God more. He says it was in large part because he was taking steps to be in closer communion with God. Mostly when God spoke he was answering questions and slowly filling in details of the path he had in store for Will and his wife. 

God was also dealing with him to bring some emotional healing. Even though it seems small, Will had to forgive himself for ways he spent his college years. He didn't do anything horrible, but he had wasted a lot of time and money after people had invested a lot in him. It had been weighing him down with guilt. 

Through prayer he was eventually able to forgive himself. When he was healed of that, God gave him a vision of armor to put on. He understood that it was like being promoted, in a way. It felt like God was now giving him more responsibility. It was a very unique set of armor that came together similar to how a turtle shell comes together. It was white and almost glowing. It was a breastplate and parts that connected to it.

This helped equip him to maneuver the long developing story God brought, and is still bringing to fruition.
Music Video Instructions: Use this as a mantra to seek God's higher love.
